task_completion.description: Create the pcap_triage folder in the Talon user directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer, terminal, or editor showing the directory C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\pcap_triage has been created.

task_completion.description: Create protocols.talon-list with the correct port mappings
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing protocols.talon-list containing 'list: user.network_protocol' and mappings for secure shell (22), web (80), secure web (443), remote desktop (3389), domain name (53), and file transfer (21).

task_completion.description: Create pcap_triage.talon with the voice command
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing pcap_triage.talon containing the voice command 'block protocol <user.network_protocol>:' followed by the action call 'user.generate_blocklist(...)'.

task_completion.description: Create pcap_triage.py with the generate_blocklist action definition
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing pcap_triage.py with a Talon Module/Context setup and a function 'def generate_blocklist(port: str):' decorated as a Talon action.

task_completion.description: Implement CSV reading and filtering logic in pcap_triage.py
task_completion.visual_evidence: Python code in pcap_triage.py showing it opens C:\workspace\data\network_traffic.csv, reads the headers, filters rows where DestinationPort matches the input port, and extracts unique SourceIPs.

task_completion.description: Implement file appending logic in pcap_triage.py
task_completion.visual_evidence: Python code in pcap_triage.py showing it opens C:\workspace\data\firewall_rules.txt in append mode ('a' or 'a+') and writes lines in the format 'deny in from <SourceIP> to any'.

integrity.description: The agent actively wrote the code files using a text editor or standard terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent typing or pasting code into an editor (like VS Code, Notepad, Vim) or using echo/cat commands, rather than the files magically appearing.

integrity.description: The Python script dynamically processes the CSV rather than hardcoding the output
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in pcap_triage.py uses variables and CSV parsing logic (e.g., csv.reader or csv.DictReader) instead of a hardcoded list of IP addresses.

integrity.description: The agent placed the files in the actual Talon AppData directory
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths visible in the editor or terminal explicitly show C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\pcap_triage, not a mock or temporary workspace directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Talon module folder: C:\Users\Docker\AppData\Roaming\talon\user\pcap_triage. CSV source path: C:\workspace\data\network_traffic.csv. Rules output path: C:\workspace\data\firewall_rules.txt.
